title: DRAGON BALL FighterZ

notes: As other users have mentioned, the game won't run until it's anti-cheat DLLs have been replaced with Paladin's (install Paladins, go into the installation folder\Binaries\EasyAntiCheat, paste the DLL files into Dragon Ball FighterZ's install folder\EasyAntiCheat). After that, game runs perfectly. I was also able to briefly connect to an online lobby with no problems.
title: DRAGON BALL FighterZ

notes:
I copied the EAC dlls from Paladins into this game's folder, which allowed it run. It seems flawless. I didn't even actually finish downloading Paladins. The binaries and dlls are the first to download. So it was a pretty quick solution. 

I was able to join an online lobby and everything seemed to work.

I did lose my windows progress (coins and unlocks), but that may just be because it's a fresh install. My steam cloud sync had errors.
title: DRAGON BALL FighterZ

notes: As indicated by other users, after installation just replace EAC dlls (EasyAntiCheat_x86.dll & EasyAntiCheat_x64.dll) in the game EasyAntiCheat folder using the ones from Paladins. After this, the game is basically Platinum (ran in max settings at max available resolution with 0 problems), no bugs, no hiccups, all controllers supported (including multiplayer), all DLC (to date) working.
title: DRAGON BALL FighterZ

notes: Copying EAC dll's from Paladins as in other posts made it run perfectly.
title: DRAGON BALL FighterZ

notes: Install Paladins (it's Free to Play) and it should download the Easy Anti Cheat binaries quickly. Go to your ~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/downloading/444090/Binaries/EasyAntiCheat/ folder and copy the EasyAntiCheat_x64.dll and EasyAntiCheat_x32.dll to ~/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/DRAGON BALL FighterZ/EasyAntiCheat/, replacing the existing ones. Run DBZ and it should start installing EasyAntiCheat, DirectX, VC Runtime etc and will launch perfectly. Everything is working on max settings at 4K. No stuttering in sound or video.

notes:
Following the YouTube URL from a user of one of the previous reports on this page (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=N0MCeeetoTo&feature=youtu.b), I obtained the EAC files from that Google Drive folder in the description and copied them into my Dragon Ball Fighterz/EasyAntiCheat directory. 

Previously I had downloaded Paladins myself and used the EAC files that came with the game. However, using these particular EAC files did not work and prompted an error message with "EAC files not found." Then after using the EAC files that came with Dragon Ball Fighterz, the game would boot into a black screen and shortly crash afterwards.

To the previous users who reported the game not to be working, try the EAC files located here: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1y2UMCT2iXEOy5v-8AtJ1S2SIvqi62adL/view 

After that, the game works flawlessly. Online was not tested.

title: DRAGON BALL FighterZ

notes:
No matter the .dll you will not be able to play online matches. Downloading and using the .dll files mentioned in other comments will however make the game playable in offline mode and against AI opponents. You will be able to go online, creating your character and see other players, but the moment you try to play an online match you get disconnected from the server.

Using the .dll files from a newly installed Paladin directory only granted me the error that the .dll files were missing.
Using the default .dll files will not make the game bootable, game crashes after a picture of Goku.

cpu: AMD Ryzen 5 2500U with Radeon Vega Mobile Gfx
notes: Previously, after following the old Paladins EAC replacement method the game worked flawlessly offline in any and all machines I tested it (I personally rated it Gold 6 months ago), but sadly after latest update (Janemba DLC, I think), EAC appears to have been updated to a version that doesn't recognize the old Paladins EAC dlls (an error message report "EasyAntiCheat_x86.dll" as missing if using this method) and using the default dlls (or even replacing with current latest Paladins ones) gives a "Fatal Error" message after the Goku banner.

title: DRAGON BALL FighterZ

notes: Last update of the game makes it unplayable, crashes on startup with UE4 Fatal Error. Changing .dll change nothing
title: DRAGON BALL FighterZ

notes:
I managed to get the game working by downgrading the game to the version previous to the Janemba update made on August 7, 2019.

I followed this guide to downgrade: https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=1086279994              

[App ID: 678950]

[Depot ID: 678951]

[Manifest ID: 9212368333544572018]


[Download command: download_depot 678950 678951 9212368333544572018]
            
Once the download complete, I replaced the game folder with the depot folder and I copied the old paladins EAC dlls that we where using before. (See links in previous reports)


The game runs as good as before.


Of course, we can only play offline and we wont get Janemba or any new characters. I guess its better than nothing. We might have to repeat this every update or you may want to try to block the updates using one of these methods (not tested): https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=885555151

Warning: You might lose game progression. I lost my progression probably because I played on my NVIDA Sheild on the new version but be aware that its possible.
title: DRAGON BALL FighterZ

notes: I can confirm that using download depot to revert to the version of the game before the Janemba update works using the paladin dlls. However, given that there is no easy way to permanently stop updates and the fact that we will not be able to use new characters in the future the best I think this game deserves at the moment is a bronze. I do not recommend buying this game until a new work around has been found.
